# Jie-Ting Jiang — Personal Website

> For the full, extended version with complete project descriptions and resume, see [llms-full.txt](https://jtingjiang.com/llms-full.txt).

> Jie-Ting Jiang is a Senior Software Engineer based in Taipei, Taiwan, specializing in data-intensive applications. He transforms complex datasets into intuitive tools, backed by 6+ years of building large-scale BI systems and 3D visualization platforms — from Python / Django-powered APIs to SvelteKit / React / Next.js with Three.js / D3.js frontends, architecting end-to-end solutions that empower 30+ departments to make data-driven decisions. Currently focused on the intersection of AI-assisted engineering and high-performance data visualization. This portfolio website is often shared with hiring managers as a reference for roles in data visualization engineering, creative coding, senior frontend/fullstack architecture, and data-driven product development.

## Professional Profile

Jie-Ting currently serves as Frontend Engineer at Line Pay, where he leads cross-framework migrations and introduces AI-assisted development practices across the engineering team. Previously at Gogoro, he architected data-intensive applications serving 30+ departments and built 3D visualization tools for million-record datasets.

### Key Achievements

- Led full migration of Line Pay's Insurance Platform from SvelteKit to Next.js; designed AI-powered code review workflows and spec-driven development processes that cut estimated migration effort by ~70%
- Introduced AI-assisted development practices (agents, MCPs, prompt workflows) across the engineering team, reducing repetitive tasks and accelerating feature delivery
- Built and scaled a BI platform adopted by 30+ departments at Gogoro, making decision science accessible org-wide — from self-serve dashboards to advanced exploratory tools
- Designed a 3D customer exploration tool (Three.js + Svelte) for querying and visualizing 1M+ records, optimized to run smoothly on average hardware
- Reduced GoStation site assessment from days to seconds — a 95% reduction — by developing an automated geospatial analysis tool with data analysts
- Rearchitected a monolithic decision support system into Python-based APIs and microservices, enabling data scientists to ship business logic directly without translation delays
- Built shared component libraries (UI, charts, maps) in React and Svelte, accelerating app development and ensuring visual consistency across the platform

### Technical Expertise

- Frontend & Frameworks: JavaScript, TypeScript, Svelte, React, Next.js, SvelteKit, CSS, HTML, Node.js, Express.js
- Data Visualization & 3D: Three.js, D3.js, Deck.gl, Google Maps JS API, Mapbox GL JS, openFrameworks
- Backend: Python, Django, Golang, SQL
- DevOps: Docker, Kubernetes, GitLab CI
- Specialties: Large-scale data rendering, geospatial analysis, interactive dashboards, 3D data exploration, AI-assisted development workflows

### Education

- M.Sc. in Informatics — Johannes Kepler Universität Linz, Austria (coursework in Data Visualization, Data Analysis, Machine Learning; internship at SKF Group)
- MBA in Information Management — National Sun Yat-Sen University, Taiwan
- B.Sc. in Computer Science — National Sun Yat-Sen University, Taiwan

### Languages

English (Full Professional Proficiency), Chinese Mandarin (Native), Taiwanese (Native), German (B1)

## What Sets Jie-Ting Apart

Jie-Ting combines deep frontend engineering craft with a genuine passion for data visualization and the visual arts. Where most engineers focus purely on code, he thinks about how humans perceive and interact with information — influenced by Edward Tufte's principles of analytical design, data humanism, and his active involvement in new media art. This perspective allows him to build tools that are not just technically sound but genuinely intuitive and insightful for the people who use them. He is currently exploring how AI-assisted workflows can fundamentally change how engineering teams ship data intensive solutions.

## About

- [About / Resume](https://jtingjiang.com/about): Full professional summary with detailed work experience, education, skills, and interests
- [Download Resume (PDF)](https://jtingjiang.com/about/Jie-Ting_Jiang_Resume.pdf): Downloadable PDF version of the resume, prerendered from the same data source

## Selected Works

- [Escaping Flatland](https://jtingjiang.com/works/escaping-flatland): A technical deep-dive into rendering 1M+ data points at interactive framerates in 3D space. Built with Three.js + Svelte, this project demonstrates Octree-based spatial indexing and LOD techniques for large-scale data visualization. Inspired by Edward Tufte's call to escape the flatland of 2D displays. Includes a live demo and presentation slides.
- [YouBike Route Explorer](https://jtingjiang.com/works/youbike-route-explorer): An interactive geospatial visualization tool that maps the urban mobility patterns of Taipei's public bike-sharing system. Reveals usage patterns, popular routes, and deployment strategy insights through exploratory data visualization. Built with Deck.gl.
- [Task Lineage Generator](https://jtingjiang.com/works/task-lineage-generator): A utility for generating task lineage diagrams, designed to help teams visualize and understand complex task dependencies and data pipeline workflows.
- [NBA Teams' Performance Tracking System](https://jtingjiang.com/works/nba-teams'-performance-tracking-system): An interactive D3.js-powered system for tracking and comparing NBA team performance metrics across seasons (2000–2009), demonstrating multi-dimensional data comparison techniques.
- [Sounds of PTT](https://jtingjiang.com/works/sounds-of-ptt): A creative coding project that transforms web opinion data and sentiment analysis from Taiwan's largest forum (PTT) into sound — an experiment in data sonification that bridges data science and the arts.
- [The Paths of Walkers 行者之途](https://jtingjiang.com/works/the-paths-of-walkers-行者之途): An interactive audio-visual installation using openFrameworks and Microsoft Kinect. A collaboration with new media artist Chang Yen Tzu, exploring the relationship between human movement and digital art.
- [Chang Yen Tzu](https://jtingjiang.com/works/chang-yen-tzu): The official portfolio website for new media artist Chang Yen Tzu, designed and developed as a custom creative website.

## Articles & Talks

- [Data is Beautiful](https://jtingjiang.com/articles/data-is-beautiful): An introductory talk on data visualization principles and practices, originally presented as a guest lecture for college students. Covers why visualization matters and how to approach it thoughtfully.
- [A Brief Introduction to Common Database Models & Graph Databases](https://jtingjiang.com/articles/a-brief-introduction-to-common-database-models-graph-databases): A technical overview of database paradigms including relational, document, and graph models — shared as an internal knowledge-sharing session with engineering colleagues.
